creator: antiduh@gmail.com
text:
The files for the pam_krb5 port are unfetchable. 

The current version of the port is looking for pam-krb5-4.3.tar.gz from  http://archives.eyrie.org/software/kerberos/. The only version of the file available from there is pam-krb5-4.4.tar.gz, suggesting that the author has released a new version and has moved the 4.3 file out of the folder. Worse, the ftp.FreeBSD.org distfiles doesn't have anything for pam_krb5 (perhaps because the port is broken?)

Snooping around, it looks like you can still download the 4.3 file (as well as all other versions) from http://archives.eyrie.org/software/ARCHIVE/pam-krb5/.

I'd suggest two fixes - one, upgrade the port to look for 4.4; two, change the port to look for the file from both locations so that the port doesn't break every time the author releases a new version.


angst(/usr/ports/security/pam_krb5) # make fetch
===>  License check disabled, port has not defined LICENSE
=> pam-krb5-4.3.tar.gz doesn't seem to exist in /usr/ports/distfiles/.
=> Attempting to fetch http://archives.eyrie.org/software/kerberos/pam-krb5-4.3.tar.gz
fetch: http://archives.eyrie.org/software/kerberos/pam-krb5-4.3.tar.gz: Not Found
=> Attempting to fetch ftp://ftp.FreeBSD.org/pub/FreeBSD/ports/distfiles/pam-krb5-4.3.tar.gz
fetch: ftp://ftp.FreeBSD.org/pub/FreeBSD/ports/distfiles/pam-krb5-4.3.tar.gz: File unavailable (e.g., file not found, no access)
=> Couldn't fetch it - please try to retrieve this
=> port manually into /usr/ports/distfiles/ and try again.
*** Error code 1

Stop in /usr/ports/security/pam_krb5.
angst(/usr/ports/security/pam_krb5) #

How-To-Repeat: Attempt to build the security/pam_krb5 port.
